resources.string: This prototype uses AI to screen banana leaf images for possible nutrient-deficiency symptoms. It is not a replacement for laboratory testing or expert agricultural advice.
This is a research prototype and NOT a validated agronomic tool. Results are for preliminary screening only.
Prepare a clear banana leaf photo before starting image screening.
Image will be processed locally on your device.
Use one banana leaf, good lighting, minimal shadow, and fill the frame with the affected leaf area.
Capture a new banana leaf photo. Images stay on this device for preview and on-device screening.
Camera access is used only to take a banana leaf photo for on-device screening. You can still choose a photo instead.
Camera access was not granted. Retry camera access, or choose a photo from your gallery.
Enable Camera permission in Android Settings to take a new photo, or choose a photo from your gallery.
Photo could not be captured. Try again, or choose a photo from your gallery.
Select one gallery image with Android Photo Picker. The app keeps only a safe image reference for this preview.
This image is ready for on-device AI screening.
Running on-device image preprocessing and model screening.
Choose a clear banana leaf image and try again.
This is an AI screening result, not a confirmed diagnosis.
Confirm with expert inspection or laboratory/soil testing.
Dataset caution: this class used maize-derived supplemental images and is not banana-validated.
This app supports classroom and research review of banana leaf nutrient-deficiency image screening.
This classroom/research prototype screens banana leaf images for possible nutrient-deficiency patterns using on-device AI.
Images are processed on this device for inference and are not uploaded by this app.

The prototype uses a MobileNetV2 LiteRT/TensorFlow Lite CPU model with 11 output classes. The result screen shows an AI screening result, a possible result, a model score, and the other top-3 possible classes.
Model score is a model output used for class ranking, not field certainty.
Model output can be uncertain and should be reviewed with field context.
Visual symptoms can overlap across nutrient stress, lighting, crop age, and image quality.
Treat the result as an AI screening result, not a field decision.
Confirm with expert inspection or laboratory/soil testing.
Do not use this prototype to choose field actions or application rates.
Shown in exact internal model label order. Internal labels are preserved for model output; display names make the screen easier to read.
Banana leaf nutrient-deficiency class in the model; interpret as a possible visual pattern only.
No visible deficiency pattern detected by this model.
Nitrogen and phosphorous used maize-derived supplemental images and are not banana-validated classes. The internal label remains phosphorous, while the UI may display Phosphorus for readability.
